title: Killing container process results in panic for podman ps --all command
body:
After experiencing the lock issue described in https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/pull/394 , I manually killed the process running inside container along with `conmon` and `runc` process.

Now if I run `podman ps --all`, it gives below panic.
```
# podman ps -a
panic: runtime error: index out of range

goroutine 1 [running]:
main.generatePsOutput(0xc42000e3c8, 0x1, 0x1, 0x1, 0x0, 0x0, 0x22d3af8, 0x57, 0xffffffffffffffff, 0x0, ...)
	/builddir/build/BUILD/libpod-3723cecc33399629408770e44eb363e9ca36fdce/_build/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/cmd/podman/ps.go:634 +0x43e
main.psCmd(0xc4200b7080, 0x0, 0x0)
	/builddir/build/BUILD/libpod-3723cecc33399629408770e44eb363e9ca36fdce/_build/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/cmd/podman/ps.go:217 +0x5a6
github.com/projectatomic/libpod/vendor/github.com/urfave/cli.HandleAction(0x1f3ae80, 0x2311138, 0xc4200b7080, 0x0, 0xc420067200)
	/builddir/build/BUILD/libpod-3723cecc33399629408770e44eb363e9ca36fdce/_build/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/vendor/github.com/urfave/cli/app.go:501 +0xd2
github.com/projectatomic/libpod/vendor/github.com/urfave/cli.Command.Run(0x225b5a0, 0x2, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x226ade3, 0xf, 0x0, ...)
	/builddir/build/BUILD/libpod-3723cecc33399629408770e44eb363e9ca36fdce/_build/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/vendor/github.com/urfave/cli/command.go:165 +0x505
github.com/projectatomic/libpod/vendor/github.com/urfave/cli.(*App).Run(0xc420012700, 0xc420010090, 0x3, 0x3, 0x0, 0x0)
	/builddir/build/BUILD/libpod-3723cecc33399629408770e44eb363e9ca36fdce/_build/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/vendor/github.com/urfave/cli/app.go:259 +0x740
main.main()
	/builddir/build/BUILD/libpod-3723cecc33399629408770e44eb363e9ca36fdce/_build/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/cmd/podman/main.go:155 +0x11a5
````

- Checking `containers.json`, I see the container `quirky_meninsky` which I killed:

comments.body:
With below `podman` version, I no longer see panic after running `podman ps -a` in above condition.
```
# podman version
Version:       0.2.3-dev
Go Version:    go1.9.4
Git Commit:    "656840c9924b0348c7e939b4215f14c5b076842f"
Built:         Fri Mar  2 09:47:14 2018
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```
So I see the panic issue is fixed.
Panic should be handled, so closing
